# Lightpanda Documentation

> Official documentation for Lightpanda headless browser — installation, quickstart guides, API reference, and cloud deployment.

## About Lightpanda

Lightpanda is a headless browser engine built from scratch in Zig for web automation, web scraping, and AI agents. It delivers 10× faster execution and 10× less memory than Chrome headless.

- [Website](https://lightpanda.io)
- [Blog](https://lightpanda.io/blog)
- [GitHub](https://github.com/lightpanda-io/browser)
- [Discord](https://discord.gg/K63XeymfB5)

## Documentation Pages (14 pages)

### Overview

- [What is Lightpanda?](https://lightpanda.io/docs/)

### Quickstart

- [Build your first data extraction script](https://lightpanda.io/docs/quickstart/build-your-first-extraction-script) — Learn how to scrape Hackernews search page.
- [Go to production with Lightpanda cloud](https://lightpanda.io/docs/quickstart/go-to-production-with-lightpanda-cloud) — Learn how to use a remote Lightpanda browser
- [Installation and setup](https://lightpanda.io/docs/quickstart/installation-and-setup) — Learn how to initialize a Node.js project using Lightpanda browser.
- [Your first test](https://lightpanda.io/docs/quickstart/your-first-test) — Learn what is Lightpanda browser and run your first scrapping script.

### Open Source

- [Build from sources](https://lightpanda.io/docs/open-source/guides/build-from-sources) — Lightpanda is written with Zig@0.14.0. You will have to install it with the right version in order to build the project.
- [Configure a proxy](https://lightpanda.io/docs/open-source/guides/configure-a-proxy) — You can configure a proxy for use with the Lightpanda browser.
- [Use Stagehand](https://lightpanda.io/docs/open-source/guides/use-stagehand) — Here is how to use Stagehand framwork with the Lightpanda browser.
- [Installation](https://lightpanda.io/docs/open-source/installation) — You can download the last binary from the nightly builds for Linux x86_64 and MacOS aarch64 or use Docker image.
- [Systems requirements](https://lightpanda.io/docs/open-source/systems-requirements) — Debian 12, Ubuntu 22.04, Ubuntu 24.04, on x86-64 and arm64 architecture. macOS 13 Ventura, or later.
- [Usage](https://lightpanda.io/docs/open-source/usage) — You can dump an URL manually or start a CDP server.

### Cloud

- [Getting started](https://lightpanda.io/docs/cloud-offer/getting-started) — Start using remote browsers easily with Lightpanda cloud.
- [CDP](https://lightpanda.io/docs/cloud-offer/tools/cdp) — Connect to Lightpanda Cloud offer using Chrome Devtool Protocol.
- [MCP](https://lightpanda.io/docs/cloud-offer/tools/mcp) — Control Lightpanda's cloud offer using Model Context Protocol
